summary: Completed backend testing of Audit Logging feature (iteration 21). All 11 tests passed (100%). Verified: 1) User invite creates audit log entry with correct details, 2) Super admin can see all audit logs across tenants, 3) Tenant admin only sees their tenant's logs (filtered by tenant_code), 4) Filter by action_type works correctly, 5) Summary endpoint returns activity statistics, 6) Role change is logged with old/new role details.

details: User invite creates audit entry with action_type=user_create
Audit entry contains target_id (user id), target_name, admin_email
Details include email, floor, room, method=invite
Super admin can retrieve all audit logs across tenants
Response includes entries, total count, pagination info
Entry structure verified with required fields: id, timestamp, admin_id, admin_email, action_type, target_type
Tenant admin only sees logs from their own tenant
Logs are filtered by tenant_code automatically
Tenant code for Ormond is ORMD0001
Filter by action_type=user_create works correctly
Filter by action_type=user_role_change works correctly
Filter by action_type=bulk_operation works correctly
Returns period_days, total_actions, actions_by_type, most_active_admins
actions_by_type is a dict with action counts
most_active_admins includes admin_id, email, actions count
Role change creates audit entry with action_type=user_role_change
Details include old_role and new_role
Severity is 'high' for role changes
Returns categorized action types
Categories: User Management, Tenant Management, Content Management, Data & Reports, Security, System
Returns current admin's own audit trail
All entries have admin_email matching current user
Super admin can access high-severity actions
Tenant admin correctly denied access (403)
All returned entries have severity=high
Pagination with skip/limit works correctly
No overlap between pages
has_more field indicates more results available

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ Audit logs correctly stored in master_db (quadley_master) for cross-tenant visibility
✓ Tenant admin filtering works correctly - only sees their tenant's logs
✓ Super admin can see all logs across tenants
✓ User invite and role change both create proper audit entries
✓ High severity actions (role changes, bulk operations) correctly marked
✓ Sensitive data sanitization in place (_sanitize_details function)
seed_data_creation: Created test users with TEST_ prefix for audit logging verification
context_for_next_testing_agent: Audit logging feature fully verified. Key findings: 1) Audit logs stored in master_db.admin_audit_log collection, 2) Tenant code for Ormond is ORMD0001, 3) All admin actions (user_create, user_role_change, bulk_operation) are properly logged, 4) Tenant admins can only see their own tenant's logs, 5) Super admins can see all logs and access high-severity endpoint. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_audit_logging.py
